evals.prompt: I'm currently using gpt-4o. What would change if I migrated to claude-sonnet-4-6?
evals.expected_output: Side-by-side diff covering pricing, context window, capabilities, SDK-level concerns (message_format, extra_headers, api_version), and rate limits, with a 2-3 sentence summary.
assertions: get_model is called twice — once for openai/gpt-4o and once for anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6
The two get_model calls are issued in parallel
The diff explicitly flags any capability gained or lost (e.g. thinking, vision, json_mode)
message_format and extra_headers deltas are surfaced as SDK-level concerns
Pricing delta is shown in both absolute ($/MTok) and percent terms

evals.prompt: Compare claude-3-5-sonnet and claude-opus-4-7 — should I migrate?
evals.expected_output: Diff plus a check against the wider claude-4 family (compare_pricing) so the user sees Sonnet 4.6 as a middle option. Recommendation includes the one thing to test first.
assertions: Both models are fetched via get_model
compare_pricing is called with model_family='claude-4' to surface adjacent Sonnet/Haiku options
The recommendation explicitly names one thing to test first before fully migrating

evals.prompt: What changes if I move from claude-sonnet-4-20250514 to claude-sonnet-4-6?
evals.expected_output: Same-format diff highlighting that message_format is unchanged but extra_headers (the prompt-caching beta header) is dropped on the newer version.
assertions: Both versions of Sonnet 4 are fetched
The diff calls out extra_headers difference even though message_format is identical
The diff does NOT claim 'no SDK change required' if extra_headers differs
